ZAKIWhy ZAKIContactGet started
Why ZAKI

Most AI answers well. Very little of it becomes a real counterpart.

ZAKI is built to close that last mile between humans and AI.

Real productivity is not about one more answer. It is about structured work now, persistent context later, and a system that becomes more useful the more it learns your operating rhythm.

That is why we built ZAKI.

Spaces keep projects organized with cleaner separation.
Works naturally across languages, including mixed-language input.
Memory keeps useful context so work continues naturally.
Designed for speed, clarity, and real daily workflows.

The product logic behind the brand.

Why ZAKI exists

Origin

Product fit is deeper than model quality

Models are shaped by language, assumptions, and the people they are built around. Useful AI is never just about model quality. It is also product fit, language fit, workflow fit, and context fit.

Gap

The real gap is continuity

Most AI still behaves like a reset button. You ask, it answers, and the working relationship starts over again. ZAKI is being built to close that last mile between people and AI.

Launch

Start with structured productivity

Spaces are the live product layer. They give users a disciplined workspace for Arabic-English daily work. That practical layer is how we earn the right to build the next product step.

Learn

Persistence changes the relationship

When context carries forward, AI becomes more useful, more trainable, and more accountable. Memory continuity, review loops, and user control matter more than one-off answers. That is what ZAKI is testing in public.

Focus

What matters operationally

Spaces keep work organized. Memory keeps continuity useful. Per-user isolation, trainability, and visible work phases make the product safer and easier to trust.

Vision

The next product step

The long-term goal is not another generic AI surface. It is persistent personal intelligence that behaves more like a digital counterpart than a one-shot tool. ZAKI is the public path toward that fuller product.

Release view

Inside ZAKI: What shaped this release

Product milestones and AI ecosystem signals that explain why ZAKI is being built this way.

⚡launchDone

ZAKI public beta is now open

The first public version of ZAKI is live with one persistent operator thread per user and visible work phases during execution.

Current releaseOpen ZAKI app
🛡️milestoneDone

Multi-user hardening and quota controls

The beta now runs with stronger per-user isolation, safer stream handling, and daily quota controls that keep the public release disciplined.

Current release
🧱ecosystemDone

OpenAI: new tools for building agents

OpenAI introduced production tooling for agents, reinforcing the shift from chatbot UX to reliable execution workflows.

Ecosystem signalRead announcement
🧭ecosystemDone

OpenAI: bridging research and action

OpenAI's ChatGPT agent launch is a clear signal: user expectations are moving from answers to actions.

Product signalRead announcement
🧩ecosystemDone

Linux Foundation launched AAIF

The Agentic AI Foundation launched with MCP, Goose, and AGENTS.md under neutral governance, accelerating interoperability across agent stacks.

Standards signalRead announcement
🧠ecosystemDone

Anthropic: context management matters

Anthropic highlighted context management as critical for long-running agents, matching ZAKI's memory-first architecture.

Engineering signalRead article
⚖️ecosystemDone

EU AI Act implementation is active

With AI Act implementation milestones active, trust, controls, and policy clarity are becoming core product requirements.

Policy signalView EU update
🛣️nextNext

Next up: deeper controls

Next, we deepen the beta with better channel controls, stronger reliability loops, and clearer operator guardrails.

Next phase
📣nextNext

Ongoing public updates

We keep publishing product progress and relevant AI signals as the product moves from disciplined chat toward persistent intelligence.

OngoingFollow updates
